name: Ponsonby
blurb: Ponsonby is one of Auckland's most sought-after inner suburbs. Older villas and character homes dominate, meaning most tradie jobs involve heritage-sensitive work — renovations, rewiring, repiling, and plumbing upgrades in pre-1940s homes.
demand: High demand for builders and electricians due to the age and character of the housing stock.

name: Mount Eden
blurb: Mount Eden and its neighbouring suburbs (Sandringham, Balmoral, Kingsland) are packed with bungalows and villas under constant renovation. It's one of Auckland's highest-volume renovation corridors.
demand: Strong demand for bathroom and kitchen renovators, plumbers, and painters.

name: Remuera
blurb: Remuera's premium housing stock drives consistent demand for high-spec tradie work. Homeowners here expect detailed quoting, tidy worksites, and quality finishes on new builds, extensions and full renovations.
demand: Premium builds and full renovations. High per-job value, discerning clients.

name: Takapuna
blurb: Takapuna on the North Shore is a mix of 1960s–1980s homes and modern apartments. Bathroom and kitchen upgrades dominate, plus roofing work on ageing homes.
demand: High demand for plumbers, bathroom renovators, roofers and painters.

name: Henderson
blurb: Henderson and West Auckland have seen significant development over the past decade. New builds, extensions and weathertight remediation work are common across Henderson, Te Atatu and New Lynn.
demand: Strong demand for builders, waterproofers and landscapers across the western corridor.

name: Newmarket
blurb: Newmarket's mix of apartments and renovated character homes creates consistent demand for plumbing, electrical and kitchen work. Proximity to Epsom and Grafton extends the service zone.
demand: Plumbing, electrical and kitchen renovation work from apartment and villa owners.

name: Parnell
blurb: Parnell is Auckland's oldest suburb and home to some of its finest villa and heritage properties. Tradie work here is often high-spec restoration — joinery, plasterwork, period-appropriate detailing.
demand: Specialist builders, painters and heritage restoration tradespeople.

name: Albany
blurb: Albany on the upper North Shore is a fast-growing hub of new builds and large-lot homes. New construction, landscaping, and fit-out work dominate the area.
demand: New build specialists, landscapers, and fit-out tradespeople.

name: Botany
blurb: Botany and East Auckland have a high concentration of family homes built in the 1990s–2010s, many now due for their first major renovation cycle — kitchens, bathrooms, decks, and fencing.
demand: Mid-cycle renovations: kitchens, bathrooms, decking, and tiling are the main jobs.

name: Onehunga
blurb: Onehunga is one of Auckland's most rapidly gentrifying suburbs. Older state homes and bungalows are being renovated or extended, with strong demand across most trades.
demand: Builders, painters, plumbers and electricians for rapid renovation volume.

name: Papakura
blurb: Papakura in South Auckland serves as a hub for tradies working across Manukau, Pukekohe and Karaka. Lifestyle blocks, new subdivisions and older homes create varied demand.
demand: Builders for new subdivisions, landscapers for lifestyle blocks, roofing and painting.

name: Manukau
blurb: Manukau is South Auckland's commercial and residential centre. High-density housing, community builds and large-lot homes create consistent tradie demand across all trades.
demand: High volume across all trades — builders, plumbers, electricians and painters.

name: Newtown
blurb: Newtown is one of Wellington's most diverse and densely populated suburbs. Its mix of 1900s villas and 1960s flats means constant demand for renovation tradies, electricians, and plumbers dealing with aging infrastructure.
demand: High demand for electricians and plumbers upgrading pre-1970s homes.

name: Karori
blurb: Karori is Wellington's largest suburb by area — a leafy residential enclave with a mix of older homes and newer builds. Strong demand for landscaping, fencing, and general building work.
demand: Strong demand for landscapers, builders and painters.

name: Johnsonville
blurb: Johnsonville is a major Wellington suburb with significant housing stock from the 1950s–1980s, driving steady demand for renovation and maintenance tradies.
demand: Steady demand for builders, roofers, and painters.

name: Petone
blurb: Petone in the Hutt Valley is one of the fastest-gentrifying suburbs in Greater Wellington. Victorian and Edwardian villas are being renovated at pace.
demand: High demand for renovation builders, electricians, and heritage plumbing work.

name: Upper Hutt
blurb: Upper Hutt's more affordable housing draws first-home buyers, many needing renovation work. Strong residential construction activity with new subdivisions opening regularly.
demand: Good demand for builders, fencers and landscapers in new-build areas.

name: Riccarton
blurb: Riccarton is Christchurch's commercial and residential heart. Post-quake rebuild activity remains strong, with both new builds and earthquake repairs keeping tradies busy.
demand: Strong demand for builders, concrete layers, and electricians.

name: Sydenham
blurb: Sydenham is a rapidly regenerating suburb south of the Christchurch CBD. Earthquake land clearances have created significant new-build activity alongside commercial redevelopment.
demand: High new-build demand for builders, plumbers and electricians.

name: Papanui
blurb: Papanui is a mature, well-established Christchurch suburb with a mix of pre-quake villas and post-quake new builds. Maintenance and renovation demand is consistent.
demand: Consistent demand for painters, roofers, and bathroom renovators.

name: Rolleston
blurb: Rolleston is one of New Zealand's fastest-growing towns, driven by Christchurch overspill. New subdivisions are booming and tradies who set up here have a ready pipeline of new-build work.
demand: Excellent new-build pipeline for builders, landscapers, and electricians.

name: Thorndon
blurb: Thorndon is Wellington's oldest suburb and home to many of the city's finest heritage homes. Work here requires care with pre-1900 villas and colonial cottages — specialist builders, painters and electricians are in constant demand.
demand: High demand for heritage builders, painters and licensed electricians.

name: Brooklyn
blurb: Brooklyn sits on Wellington's southern hills with stunning harbour views and a mix of 1920s–1970s homes. Steep sections mean most work involves specialist knowledge of hillside construction, retaining walls and drainage.
demand: Strong demand for builders, retaining wall specialists, and painters.

name: Island Bay
blurb: Island Bay is a popular southern Wellington suburb with older homes and active renovation. Its coastal position also drives demand for painting, weatherproofing and exterior maintenance.
demand: Strong demand for exterior painters, plumbers and builders.

name: Lower Hutt
blurb: Lower Hutt is the Hutt Valley's main city, with a large stock of 1950s–1980s homes undergoing renovation. Strong across all trades with growing new-build activity in surrounding areas.
demand: High volume across all trades — builders, plumbers, electricians and roofers.

name: Burnside
blurb: Burnside is a well-established Christchurch suburb popular with families. Post-quake rebuild work is winding down and renovation demand is taking over — bathrooms, kitchens and landscaping lead.
demand: Good demand for kitchen and bathroom renovators, landscapers and painters.

name: Merivale
blurb: Merivale is one of Christchurch's most prestigious inner suburbs. Premium renovation work, character home restorations and high-spec landscaping dominate. Tradies here can command above-average rates.
demand: Premium renovation and landscaping with high per-job value.

name: Linwood
blurb: Linwood east of the CBD was heavily impacted by the 2011 earthquakes. Significant new-build and repair activity continues, alongside older homes still awaiting renovation.
demand: High demand for builders, concreters and electricians for ongoing rebuild work.

name: North Shore
blurb: Auckland's North Shore spans Devonport to Albany and is home to thousands of 1970s–1990s homes entering major renovation cycles. Demand is high across all trades, with bathroom and kitchen upgrades leading.
demand: High volume across all trades — plumbers, tilers, painters and electricians.

name: East Auckland
blurb: East Auckland covers Howick, Pakuranga and Botany Downs — a large area of 1980s–2000s family homes, many now due for their first full renovation. Bathroom and kitchen upgrades are the main driver.
demand: Strong demand for tilers, bathroom renovators, kitchen fitters and landscapers.
